2605.23826.title: Decomposing Queries into Tool Calls for Long-Video Keyframe Retrieval
2605.23826.paper_summary: Keyframe selection is a direct way to provide verifiable visual evidence for long-video question answering (QA). Queries differ in what they require, and finding the right frames depends on knowing what to look for. Existing keyframe selectors either score every frame against a single query, or decompose the query into a fixed schema evaluated by a single visual tool. We propose ToolMerge, a keyframe retrieval method based on decomposition and merging: an Large Language Model (LLM) based planner decomposes the query into tool calls and specifies how their per-tool rankings are merged using boolean operators. To evaluate retrieval directly, we construct Molmo-2 Moments (M2M), a benchmark in which every question is anchored to a specific time interval by construction. Across QA, question retrieval, and caption retrieval, ToolMerge is competitive with prior keyframe selectors, most notably on caption retrieval, outperforming other methods by 5%. Code and data can be found at https://github.com/michalsr/ToolMerge .
2605.23826.paper_summary_zh: 關鍵幀選擇是一種直接提供可驗證視覺證據的方式，用於長視頻的問題回答（QA）。查詢的要求各不相同，而找到合適的幀取決於知道要尋找什麼。現有的關鍵幀選擇器要麼對每個幀進行單一查詢的評分，要麼將查詢分解為由單一視覺工具評估的固定架構。我們提出了 ToolMerge，一種基於分解和合併的關鍵幀檢索方法：一個基於大型語言模型（LLM）的規劃器將查詢分解為工具調用，並指定如何使用布爾運算符合併其每個工具的排名。為了直接評估檢索，我們構建了 Molmo-2 Moments (M2M)，這是一個基準，其中每個問題都通過構造固定在特定時間區間內。在問題回答、問題檢索和標題檢索方面，ToolMerge 與先前的關鍵幀選擇器具有競爭力，尤其是在標題檢索上，超過其他方法 5%。代碼和數據可在 https://github.com/michalsr/ToolMerge 獲得。
2605.23826.author: Michal Shlapentokh-Rothman et.al.
2605.23826.authors: Michal Shlapentokh-Rothman, Prachi Garg, Yu-Xiong Wang, Derek Hoiem
